summary:
Only "P pulmonale" has been clearly identified through textual linkage between its defining ECG pattern—the presence of tall P waves in leads II or V1—and pathophysiology of right-sided cardiac dysfunction caused by chronic pressure overload leading to pulmonary vascular disease. This makes it uniquely aligned both structurally and conceptually with available descriptive material while excluding alternatives lacking explicit documentation or relevance according to stated criteria.

The most appropriate answer remains \boxed{C}, reflecting recognition that tall P waves specifically indicate right atrial enlargement/strain typically seen in conditions causing elevated pulmonary pressures—a hallmark feature recognized globally in cardiological practice when interpreting standard resting ECG tracings.

We now proceed forward confidently knowing our analysis aligns strictly with documented associations present throughout the source materials reviewed during initial evaluation phase.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$
